threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: Calling everything ‘the tube’ instead of the ‘Overground’ shows you’re still finding your feet. We’re all about those connections, mate.
Caught the N159 last week; a couple was arguing about whether pie and mash counts as fine dining. Only in Brixton at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a street musician playing an Arabic oud while a group of kids skateboarded past, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find a bar where they serve beer in recycled mason jars, and the beer is brewed right next door. Only in Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can order a burrito at La Taqueria without looking at the menu, and they definitely know to ask for extra jalapeños.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy balancing on a skateboard while jamming on a sax in the back of a crowded Muni bus. Only in the Mission, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think the local shops are overpriced, but many owners still offer discounts if you just ask. They appreciate genuine engagement.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll often see them snapping photos of every building with intricate façades, oblivious to the locals bustling past on their bikes.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'Clapton Station' instead of just 'Clapton' like the rest of us. It's a subtle tell, but we know.
Caught a glimpse of a couple arguing over a lost shoe on the N21 last night—turns out it was on a dodgy roof two stops back.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a middle-aged woman negotiating with a street musician for a song request – both laughing like old friends.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it's not uncommon to see party bikers sharing the road with grocery delivery trikes—only in Berlin can you sip a beer while getting your groceries delivered!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their disdain for the 14-Mission after 9 p.m. They know better than to get stuck there waiting for a bus that never comes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Nothing like the Muni bus pulling up just as the street performers start a set—always a distraction, but it’s part of the Mission vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just ask for ‘tap water’ in restaurants, but in Gràcia, it's more common to order a ‘copa d'aigua.’ Takes a while to figure that one out.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll see them holding a paper map of the city, looking bewildered at the intersections, while the locals swipe their phones to navigate.
